card_description: Elijah (Hebrew: "My God is Yahweh") is a divine or mythological figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the biblical tradition, Elijah is prophet who ascended to heaven in a chariot of fire. PKD identified with Elijah as a prophetic voice; connected to fiery ascent imagery. Appears in 22 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Elijah. Context: "Dick explores "Immanence" using Parmenides' "All" and Kozyrev's time theory, concluding that God is total in every "micropoint."
